title: Market Brief — Thursday, May 7, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100 at 70%, MI300X at 60%, while H200 drops to 45% and B200 at just 25% signals scarcity on next-gen silicon. GB200 at 10% availability is in severe shortage with no reliable spot pricing, confirming Blackwell Ultra demand far outstrips supply. The biggest price dislocation is H200 OCPI at $4.13/hr versus $3.00 spot median, a 38% premium on trade-based pricing with 86.3% annualized volatility.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100 at 70%, MI300X at 60%, while H200 drops to 45% and B200 at just 25% signals scarcity on next-gen silicon. GB200 at 10% availability is in severe shortage with no reliable spot pricing, confirming Blackwell Ultra demand far outstrips supply. The biggest price dislocation is H200 OCPI at $4.13/hr versus $3.00 spot median, a 38% premium on trade-based pricing with 86.3% annualized volatility.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $9.84/M tokens, meaning any provider pricing output at $6/M or below is burning cash on that hardware. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M are selling at catastrophic losses exceeding $9/M tokens on legacy GPUs. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash, xAI Burning Cash.

B200 is undervalued: TCO-adjusted inference cost of $5.03/M tokens is 49% cheaper than H100-equivalent, yet B200 spot pricing hasn't fully corrected, creating the best margin opportunity for builders willing to lock capacity at 25% availability. H200 is overvalued at $4.13 OCPI with utilization dropping 10.1 percentage points in 30 days; sell H200 capacity commitments and rotate into B200 before scarcity pricing kicks in.
bullets: 25% B200 availability marks the tightest supply of any priced GPU class, one notch above severe shortage.
10% GB200 availability qualifies as severe shortage with zero spot median established.
$80/M output tokens makes OpenAI o3 Pro the most expensive model tracked, 211x pricier than DeepSeek V3.2.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API pricing has fallen roughly 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline.
$9.52/M TCO-adjusted inference cost on MI300X makes AMD the most expensive path to run Llama 70B, a $3.52/M penalty versus B200.
95.6% H100 utilization, up 9.4 points in 30 days, signals demand is absorbing remaining oversupply fast.
